meta_title: Impact Doors in Sheridan, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Sheridan, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph wind-rated, in-house install. Get a free quote today!
h1: Impact Doors in Sheridan, FL — Fortify Your Home Against South Florida Storms
intro:
Sheridan may sit quietly between Hollywood, Pembroke Pines, and Davie, but its location in Broward County puts it squarely in the path of some of the most powerful storms the Atlantic basin produces. With a 170mph wind zone designation and a storm history that includes the catastrophic Hurricane Andrew, the lingering destruction of Hurricane Wilma, and the widespread damage of Hurricane Irma, Sheridan homeowners have every reason to take hurricane protection seriously. Impact doors are no longer a luxury upgrade — they are a practical, code-required investment in the safety of your family and the long-term value of your home.

The residential neighborhoods surrounding Sheridan Park represent a broad cross-section of Broward County housing stock. Approximately half of the homes in this ZIP code (33021) were built before 1994, which means they predate the sweeping Florida Building Code reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew. Those older homes were designed to far less rigorous standards than what we understand today about wind-driven rain, structural uplift, and impact resistance. Replacing aging entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors with modern, impact-rated alternatives is one of the single most effective upgrades an older Sheridan home can receive — both for storm resilience and for protecting home values that average around $350,000 in this area.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've spent decades helping Broward County homeowners make this essential transition. Our impact doors don't just meet the minimum requirements set by the Broward County Building Division — they exceed them. Whether you're updating a classic ranch-style home near Sheridan Park or modernizing a split-level in one of the established residential pockets of this community, our team brings the local knowledge, technical expertise, and brand-name products to get the job done right the first time. We are, after all, the Kings of Service.

section_why.h2: Why Sheridan Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation in Sheridan isn't just about finding the lowest price — it's about finding a team with the experience, licensing, and local accountability to protect one of your most valuable assets. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined installation experience to every project we take on in Broward County. Our team has completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we understand the specific code requirements, wind zone conditions, and permitting expectations that apply to Sheridan residents.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart from many competitors is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract. Every door installation is performed by our own trained, licensed technicians who have been vetted, trained, and held to our internal quality standards. This means you're never dealing with a third-party crew that has no relationship with the company you hired. From measurement and product selection to final inspection and permit closeout, the same team handles every step. That continuity of care is rare in the home improvement industry, and it's one of the reasons we've earned an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau.

We also understand that Sheridan's housing stock presents unique challenges. Older pre-1994 construction often requires frame reinforcement, sill pan modifications, or threshold adjustments that a less experienced crew might overlook. Our technicians are trained to identify and address these structural nuances before they become problems — not after. We pull all required permits through the Broward County Building Division, coordinate inspections, and ensure your new impact doors are fully compliant and documented. That documentation matters when it comes time to file for wind mitigation credits with your insurance carrier or when you eventually sell your home.

For Sheridan families investing in a home worth $350,000 or more, cutting corners on installation simply isn't an option. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing treats your home the way we'd treat our own — with precision, pride, and a commitment to results that stand up to whatever the Atlantic hurricane season throws at South Florida next.

section_history.h2: Storm History Reminds Sheridan Residents That the Threat Is Real and Recurring
section_history.body:
Since 1990, Sheridan and the broader Broward County region have been impacted by nine named storms — a sobering statistic that underscores just how active this stretch of South Florida coastline truly is. Three storms in particular have left lasting impressions on the local community and reshaped how homeowners think about hurricane preparedness. Hurricane Andrew in 1992, one of the most destructive storms in U.S. history, exposed catastrophic weaknesses in residential construction across South Florida. While Andrew's core struck further south in Miami-Dade, its outer bands and the structural vulnerabilities it revealed affected homes throughout Broward County, including communities like Sheridan. The building code reforms that followed Andrew in 1994 were a direct response to the widespread damage caused by inadequate doors, windows, and roofing systems.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 was a more direct threat to Broward County. Wilma crossed South Florida as a Category 3 storm, producing sustained winds that overwhelmed many homes that had not yet been upgraded to post-Andrew standards. Sliding glass doors, which are common in Sheridan's older ranch-style homes, were among the most frequently damaged entry points. When a sliding glass door fails in a storm, it doesn't just let in wind — it allows interior pressure to build rapidly, which can lead to roof uplift and catastrophic structural damage. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which reinforced once again that no part of Broward County is immune to storm surge, high winds, and the kind of wind-driven rain that penetrates even well-maintained but non-impact-rated doors.

These storms are not ancient history. Many current Sheridan homeowners lived through one or more of them. Impact doors represent the clearest, most proven path to ensuring that the next major storm — and there will be a next one — does not compromise your home, your belongings, or your family's safety.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Sheridan and Broward County
section_code.body:
Sheridan falls within Broward County's jurisdiction for building permits and code compliance. While this community is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which is limited to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal communities meeting specific criteria, it is still subject to rigorous wind-resistance requirements under the Florida Building Code (FBC). Homes in the 33021 ZIP code are designated within a 170mph wind speed zone, which means all exterior openings — including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — must be protected by products tested and rated to withstand that level of wind pressure.

The Florida Building Code requires that replacement impact doors in Sheridan meet specific Large Missile Impact (LMI) test standards, which simulate the force of wind-borne debris during a hurricane. Products must carry a Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Product Approval from an accredited testing laboratory, and they must be installed in strict accordance with the manufacturer's approved installation drawings. Deviating from those specifications — even minor deviations like incorrect fastener spacing — can void the product's approval and result in a failed inspection.

All impact door installations in Sheridan require permits pulled through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this permitting process entirely on your behalf. We prepare the documentation, submit the applications, coordinate the inspection schedule, and ensure your project reaches final approval without delays. This is not a step you want to skip. Unpermitted impact door work not only creates liability if you sell your home, it can also result in your insurance carrier denying wind mitigation credits or even coverage claims. Doing it right — with proper permits, approved products, and licensed installation — is the only way to maximize both your protection and your investment.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Built for Broward County's Wind Zone
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we install impact doors from two of the industry's most trusted brands: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers produce impact-rated door systems that have been rigorously tested and certified to meet Florida Building Code requirements, including the 170mph wind zone standards applicable to Sheridan. These are not off-the-shelf doors dressed up with marketing language — they are purpose-engineered hurricane protection systems designed specifically for South Florida's demanding climate conditions.

ESW impact doors are built with heavy-duty aluminum or reinforced PVCu frames and feature laminated impact glass that holds together even when struck by wind-borne debris traveling at hurricane speeds. Their entry door systems are available in a wide range of styles and finishes, making them an excellent choice for Sheridan homeowners who want storm protection without sacrificing curb appeal. ESW sliding glass doors are particularly popular in older Broward County homes, where standard aluminum sliders are among the most common points of failure during storm events. The ESW sliding door systems feature multi-point locking mechanisms that engage at multiple points along the door frame, dramatically increasing resistance to wind pressure compared to traditional single-point latches.

CWI (Custom Windows & Interiors) rounds out our impact door lineup with a strong selection of French door systems and entry door configurations that appeal to homeowners seeking a more traditional or architectural aesthetic. CWI products feature the same laminated safety glass construction and multi-point locking systems as ESW, and they carry full Florida Product Approval certifications. Whether you're replacing a dated single entry door, upgrading a pair of French doors opening to a back patio, or modernizing a large sliding glass door that faces your pool deck, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's product lineup gives Sheridan homeowners the right solution for every opening in their home.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Sheridan — and How to Make the Investment Work for Your Budget
section_cost.body:
For Sheridan homeowners with properties averaging around $350,000, impact door installation represents one of the highest-return upgrades available. The cost of impact doors varies depending on the number of openings being replaced, the door type (entry, sliding glass, or French), the size of the opening, and the specific product and finish selected. As a general benchmark, a single impact entry door installation in Broward County typically ranges from $1,500 to $4,000 installed, while large sliding glass door systems or multi-panel French door configurations can range from $3,000 to $8,000 or more per opening. Homes with multiple doors to replace can often take advantage of project bundling, which reduces per-door costs and streamlines the permitting and inspection process.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this investment accessible to Sheridan families at every income level through flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down required. Our financing programs are designed to let you move forward with the protection your home needs today, without waiting until you've saved the full project cost out of pocket. Given that a single hurricane season can bring multiple named storms, delaying impact door installation for financial reasons is a risk that carries very real consequences.

Beyond the upfront cost, the return on investment for impact doors in Sheridan is compelling. Most Broward County homeowners who upgrade to impact doors see meaningful reductions in their homeowners' insurance premiums through wind mitigation credits — savings that can offset a significant portion of the monthly financing cost. Impact doors also add measurable resale value to your home, improve energy efficiency by reducing air infiltration around door frames, and eliminate the annual hassle and cost of purchasing, storing, and installing hurricane shutters. When you factor in all of these benefits, impact doors are not simply a safety expense — they are a smart financial decision for any Sheridan homeowner.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Benefits for Sheridan Impact Door Installations
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact doors in Sheridan is the potential for significant homeowners' insurance savings. Florida's insurance market has been under considerable stress in recent years, with many carriers raising premiums dramatically or reducing coverage in Broward County. However, homeowners who take documented steps to improve their wind resistance — including installing impact-rated doors and windows — are entitled to wind mitigation credits under Florida Statute 627.0629, which requires insurance carriers to offer discounts for verified hurricane-hardening improvements.

To access these discounts, Sheridan homeowners need a wind mitigation inspection performed by a licensed inspector who documents the presence of impact-rated openings using a standardized OIR-B1-1802 form. When your impact doors are installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing with proper permits and final inspections, all the documentation needed for that wind mitigation report is already in place. Common insurance carriers serving Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and several others — apply opening protection credits that can reduce the wind portion of your premium by 15% to 45% or more, depending on the number of openings protected and your specific policy structure.

For a middle-income Sheridan homeowner paying $4,000 to $7,000 or more per year in homeowners' insurance — which is increasingly common in Broward County — those percentage savings translate to hundreds of dollars annually in real premium reductions. Over the 10 to 20-year lifespan of your impact doors, the cumulative insurance savings alone can offset a meaningful portion of the original installation cost. Combined with the elimination of annual shutter costs, enhanced home security from multi-point locking systems, and the added resale value of a fully hurricane-protected home, the financial case for impact doors in Sheridan is as strong as the storms that make them necessary.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Sheridan if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Sheridan is approximately 4 miles from the coast and sits in a 170mph wind zone designated by the Florida Building Code. Wind speeds at that level do not require coastal proximity to cause catastrophic damage to standard doors and windows. The storm histories of Hurricane Andrew, Wilma, and Irma all demonstrated that inland Broward County communities experience severe wind damage. Impact doors provide protection regardless of how far you are from the water.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors typically cost in Sheridan, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: In Sheridan, impact door installations typically range from $1,500 to $4,000 for a single entry door and $3,000 to $8,000+ for larger sliding glass or French door systems, depending on size and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down through our flexible financing programs. We'll help you build a project scope that fits your budget while delivering the protection your home needs.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for impact door replacement in Sheridan, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes, all impact door replacements in Sheridan require permits issued through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — we prepare documentation, submit applications, and coordinate required inspections. This ensures your installation is fully code-compliant, properly documented, and eligible for insurance wind mitigation credits. Never allow a contractor to install impact doors without pulling the required permits.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a typical Sheridan home?
faqs.a: Most impact door installations in Sheridan are completed in one to two days for a typical single-family home, depending on the number of openings being replaced. Permitting, which Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages for you, typically takes one to three weeks depending on current Broward County Building Division processing times. We'll provide a clear timeline during your initial consultation and keep you informed at every step of the project.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowners' insurance with impact doors in Broward County?
faqs.a: Sheridan homeowners who install impact-rated doors and document them through a licensed wind mitigation inspection can qualify for opening protection credits that reduce the wind portion of their premium by 15% to 45% or more, depending on the carrier and policy. With Broward County insurance premiums frequently exceeding $4,000 to $7,000 per year, that translates to hundreds — sometimes thousands — of dollars in annual savings that meaningfully offset the cost of your impact door investment over time.
